channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ⚔️ Israel and Iran Halt Strikes After Trump Warning
item.description: Israel and Iran paused military strikes following a direct appeal from US President Donald Trump, who warned Netanyahu that Israel would be "on your own" if it resumed attacks. The temporary ceasefire comes after months of conflict that has disrupted global oil markets.

item.title: ☢️ IAEA Urges Iran to Re-Engage on Nuclear Inspections
item.description: UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi called on Iran to allow inspections at sites bombed by the US and Israel. The US led a push for a resolution at the IAEA board demanding Iran resume cooperation.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Edge Higher Amid Iran-Israel Uncertainty
item.description: Oil prices rose slightly as markets awaited clarity on whether the Iran-Israel halt would hold. The Strait of Hormuz remains a focal point, with Iran's envoy saying transit fees would apply.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Says Israel Bombed It Nearly 3,500 Times During Ceasefire
item.description: Israel conducted nearly 3,500 airstrikes on Lebanon since the April ceasefire announcement, Lebanon's defense minister said. Lebanese President Aoun appealed directly to Israel to pursue negotiations instead of war.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping Visits North Korea for First Summit in Seven Years
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for his first visit in seven years, meeting with Kim Jong Un. The summit follows Putin's recent visit and signals deepening coordination between China and North Korea.

item.title: 🇲🇾 Malaysia opposition in turmoil as Islamist party PAS cuts ties with Bersatu
item.description: PAS ended its cooperation with Bersatu, gutting the Perikatan Nasional opposition coalition ahead of key elections in Malaysia.

item.title: 🌏 Asian stocks track Wall Street tech bounce as oil eases on Mideast ceasefire hopes
item.description: Asian markets rebounded as investors returned to AI stocks while crude prices eased following Israel-Iran hostilities pause.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Names First Female Prime Minister in Decades to Lead AI Push
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung nominated former Naver CEO Han Seong-sook as prime minister, tasking her with driving South Korea's AI-driven economic transformation.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Prepares 21st Sanctions Package Against Russia Worth $1.5 Trillion
item.description: The European Union is finalizing its 21st sanctions package against Russia, targeting additional sectors to increase economic pressure on Moscow.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Blames US for Latest Exchanges of Fire with Israel
item.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry says overnight strikes will worsen an already chaotic diplomatic process with Washington, exchanging messages in an atmosphere of extreme suspicion.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Seeks Calm with Ukraine Over WWII Army Unit Dispute
item.description: Polish PM Tusk calls for solidarity and talks after diplomatic relations deteriorated over Ukraine naming an army unit for nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles in World War Two.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi and Kim Agree to Expand Cooperation at Pyongyang Summit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un held a rare summit in Pyongyang, agreeing to expand cooperation in politics, economy and culture.

item.title: 📉 Gulf Stock Markets Drop Amid Renewed Middle East Tensions
item.description: Most stock markets in the Gulf ended lower on Monday as renewed conflict in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment.

item.title: 🇺🇸🛢️ US Military Disables Oil Tanker Headed to Iran in Gulf of Oman
item.description: US forces disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman as the unladen vessel headed toward Iran, escalating enforcement of the US naval blockade amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷🚢 Iran Threatens to Defeat US Naval Blockade as Tensions Persist
item.description: Iran top negotiator vowed to turn the US naval blockade into another defeat for the enemy, as Tehran faces increased maritime interdiction of its oil exports during the ongoing conflict with Israel.

item.title: 🇺🇸📜 US Judge Rules Trump H-1B Visa Fee Unlawful
item.description: A federal judge declared the $100,000 fee imposed by President Trump on new H-1B visas for highly skilled workers unlawful, dealing a blow to the administration immigration restrictions.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon reports nearly 3,500 Israeli strikes during ceasefire period
item.description: Lebanon's defense minister says Israel carried out nearly 3,500 airstrikes and hundreds of controlled explosions since the U.S.-announced ceasefire on April 16, highlighting continued violence despite formal truce.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine recaptures over 600 square kilometers of territory in 2026
item.description: Ukraine's military chief reports retaking more than 600 sq km this year, with independent battlefield mappers confirming Russia's advances have slowed or reversed in recent months.

item.title: ⛽ Russia's fuel crisis deepens as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories
item.description: Ukrainian attacks on Russian supply lines have made it difficult for Moscow to provide military and civilian supplies to occupied territories, intensifying pressure on Russian logistics.

item.title: ⚖️ ICC Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan Suspended Over Sexual Misconduct Allegations
item.description: The International Criminal Court's oversight body suspended Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan late Monday, referring him for disciplinary proceedings. Khan faces allegations of sexual misconduct with a female aide spanning more than two years, which he denies.

item.title: ☢️ Nuclear Weapons Spending Hits Record $119 Billion as Arms Race Intensifies
item.description: Global spending on nuclear weapons rose 19% in 2025 to $119 billion, the highest since tracking began. The US led with $69.2 billion, more than all other nuclear states combined, while China increased spending by 7% to $13.5 billion.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping Visits North Korea for First Time in Seven Years, Pledges Strategic Cooperation
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping met with Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang, agreeing to expand bilateral cooperation. Kim pledged full support for the "One China principle" on Taiwan, while Xi called for deepening strategic coordination between the two nations.

item.title: 🐄 Flesh-Eating Screwworm Parasite Spreads Beyond Texas, Canada Blocks Livestock Imports
item.description: The USDA confirmed three new screwworm cases including one outside Texas, in New Mexico. The flesh-eating parasite poses a threat to the cattle industry, with five confirmed cases so far including calves, a goat, and a dog.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky Holds "Very Positive" Call With US Envoys Witkoff and Kushner
item.description: Ukrainian President discussed Moscow's intentions and peace efforts with Trump's envoys in a positive phone call amid ongoing negotiations.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu Openly Defies Trump With Iran Strike Amid Lebanon Pressure
item.description: Israeli PM's decision to strike Iran despite Trump's ceasefire demands highlights growing rift and Israel's attempt to secure influence in peace negotiations.
